Pyrimidine-conjugated fluoroquinolones as new potential broad-spectrum
antibacterial agents
Yi-Min Tana,1, Di Lia,1, Fen-Fen Lia,1, Mohammad Fawad Ansaria,*, Bo Fangb,*,
Cheng-He Zhoua,*
aInstitute of Bioorganic & Medicinal Chemistry, Key Laboratory of Applied Chemistry of Chongqing Municipality, School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering,
Southwest University, Chongqing 400715, PR China
bCollege of Pharmacy, National & Local Joint Engineering Research Center of Targeted and Innovative Therapeutics, Chongqing Key Laboratory of Kinase Modulators as
Innovative Medicine, Chongqing University of Arts and Sciences, Chongqing 402160, PR China
A R T I C L E I N F O A B S T R A C T
Keywords: Pyrimidine-conjugated fluoroquinolones were constructed to cope with the dreadful resistance. Most of the
Pyrimidine target pyrimidine derivatives effectively suppressed the growth of the tested strains, especially, 4-aminopyrimi-
Fluoroquinolone dinyl compound 1c showed a broad antibacterial spectrum and low cytotoxicity and exhibited superior anti-
Antibacterial bacterial potency against Enterococcus faecalis with a low MIC of 0.25 μg/mL to norfloxacin and ciprofloxacin.
DNA
The active compound 1c with fast bactericidal potency could inhibit the formation of biofilms and showed much
lower trend for the development of drug-resistance than norfloxacin and ciprofloxacin. Further exploration
revealed that compound 1c could prompt ROS accumulations in bacterial cells and interact with DNA to form a
DNA-1c complex, thus facilitating bacterial death. ADME analysis indicated that compound 1c possessed
favorable drug-likeness and promising pharmacokinetic properties. These results demonstrated that pyrimidine-
conjugated fluoroquinolones held hope as potential antibacterial candidates and deserve further study.
Quinolones are one of the most important first-line antibacterial norfloxacin and ciprofloxacin, so piperazine is always utilized as a

protection of human health.2 However, due to the over-extensive clin- imidazole,8 triazole,9 thiazole10 and benzimidazole11 were introduced

otics is increasing, leading to a decline in clinical efficacy.3 Structural molecules with excellent antibacterial potential. Pyrimidine as a unique

important approaches to combat this urgency. Much work about the drugs12 especially in antibacterial sulfonamides such as sulfadiazine,

extensively modified by some unique functional groups due to large dines with quinolones is rarely reported,13 thus the research on intro-

resistant development tendency of wild-type bacteria and enhancing needs to be vigorously promoted.

atom in the quinolone skeleton can further increase the tissue perme- 7 position of fluoroquinolones, it is very interested in combining

Another commercial fluoroquinolone ingredient ciprofloxacin was aminopyrimidine-conjugated quinolone 1c displayed a broad antibac-

terial mechanism.
The persistence and refractory of bacterial infection caused by drug
resistance pose an indelible impact on global health,15 and hence, the
resistance-induced tendency of candidates is an important evaluation
indicator for being developed and listed.16 The propensity of drug
resistance induced by aminopyrimidine-conjugated quinolone 1c to-
wards E. faecalis was evaluated using norfloxacin and ciprofloxacin as
positive controls, and the development of drug resistance was displayed
in Fig. 4. The MICs of compound 1c against E. faecalis remained stable
within 11 days and merely occurred a very low enhancement at the end
of evaluation, which manifested that compound 1c possessed a low
tendency to trigger the development of resistance. However, the fold-
change in the MICs of both clinical norfloxacin and ciprofloxacin
showed 7-fold increases, suggesting that the bacterial resistances caused
by norfloxacin and ciprofloxacin were significant. The low trend in the
development of E. faecalis resistance to aminopyrimidine-conjugated
Fig. 1. Design of pyrimidine-conjugated fluoroquinolones as antibacte- quinolone 1c was another advantage in addition to its strong antibac-
rial agents. terial activity, being compared to available antibacterial drugs.

fluorescence intensity with respect to the control group. These results Further study on the interactive approach between
aminopyrimidine-conjugated quinolone 1c and DNA was carried out by
competitive experiments with acridine orange (AO),28 and the result
was shown in Fig. 9. An obvious reduction was observed in the fluo-
rescence intensity of AO-DNA complex around 530 nm with the con-
centration of hybrid 1c increasing, indicating that aminopyrimidinyl
derivative 1c could inset into DNA by replacing AO in the AO-DNA
complex. Moreover, an intuitively docking simulation was exhibited in
Fig. S2, which provided evidence for the intercalation of compound 1c
into DNA. All these exposed the change in DNA duplexes and the for-
mation of DNA (cid:0) 1c supramolecular complex, thus aminopyrimidine-
conjugated quinolone 1c exerting more potent antibacterial potency.
Bacterial DNA gyrase as an exact target of quinolones plays an
important role in the normal physiological function of DNA.29 Given the
interaction between aminopyrimidine-conjugated quinolone 1c and
DNA, a docking simulation between compound 1c and DNA gyrase (PDB
code: 4HZ5) was implemented to further determine the interaction site
between molecule 1c and DNA. Fig. 10A showed that three oxygen
atoms of 3-carboxyl and 4-carbonyl moieties could bind with residue
LYS-202 through hydrogen bonds with the distance of 2.2, 2.5 and 2.4 Å.
Meanwhile, it was observed that there was a hydrogen bond between the
nitrogen atom at the 3-position of pyrimidine and residue GLY-203. The
Fig. 6. E. faecalis biofilm inhibition by aminopyrimidine-conjugated quino-
hydrogen atom of NH2 group possessed a hydrogen bond with residue
GLY-203 at a distance of 2.3 Å, which could be responsible for the
lone 1c.
